title: Subgroup operations + workgroup barrier
goal: Expose portable subgroup/wave-level primitives (reductions, broadcast, ballot) and workgroup barriers via SPV_KHR_shader_subgroup_* extensions. Enables per-warp/wave reductions, prefix scan primitives, and warp-cooperative work. Unlocks real parallel algorithms beyond single-threaded reductions.
scope_in: Subgroup builtins: subgroup_invocation_id() -> u32, subgroup_size() -> u32, subgroup_elect() -> bool
Subgroup reductions: subgroup_reduce_add(T) -> T, subgroup_reduce_min/max(T) -> T for T in {i32, u32, f32, f64}
Subgroup broadcast: subgroup_broadcast_first(T) -> T (broadcasts from first active invocation)
Subgroup ballot: subgroup_ballot(bool) -> u32x4 (4-lane bitmask — AXIOM uses uvec4); subgroup_all(bool) -> bool; subgroup_any(bool) -> bool
Workgroup barrier: workgroup_barrier() — OpControlBarrier with Workgroup execution scope + AcquireRelease|WorkgroupMemory semantics

Capabilities emitted lazily per usage: GroupNonUniform (invocation_id, size, elect, ballot, broadcast_first), GroupNonUniformArithmetic (reduce), GroupNonUniformBallot (ballot/any/all), GroupNonUniformShuffle (broadcast_first requires this on some drivers; or use GroupNonUniformBallot path)
Extensions emitted lazily: SPV_KHR_shader_subgroup_basic, SPV_KHR_shader_subgroup_arithmetic, SPV_KHR_shader_subgroup_ballot
HIR type checking: every subgroup op rejects non-numeric / non-scalar types with a clear error
HIR constraint: subgroup ops must be in 'convergent' context (i.e., not inside divergent control flow) — for M1.4, we ACCEPT with a warning; strict enforcement deferred to M1.5
Example: examples/subgroup_reduce.axc — per-subgroup sum of buffer, one thread per subgroup writes result
scope_out: Atomic ops (M1.5 bundled with Vulkan dispatch)
Subgroup shuffle by lane id (broadcast_first only for M1.4)
Subgroup voting (vote_all, vote_any — equivalent to _all/_any, just naming)
Divergent control flow validation — deferred with a warning
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace passes (floor 380 tests after M1.4)
In-process spirv-tools validates every emitted kernel (no regressions from M1.3)
subgroup_reduce.axc compiles and validates for Vulkan 1.1
Capability + extension pairs emit correctly for each subgroup op used
Workgroup barrier emits OpControlBarrier with correct memory semantics (AcquireRelease | WorkgroupMemory = 0x8 | 0x100 = 0x108)
open_questions_for_architect: Subgroup Execution Scope: spec mandates Subgroup (3) for all OpGroupNonUniform* ops. Confirm uint32 value.
Subgroup reduce operation: SPIR-V defines OpGroupNonUniformIAdd / OpGroupNonUniformFAdd / etc. Operation scope = Reduce (2) vs InclusiveScan/ExclusiveScan — M1.4 uses Reduce only.

Ballot result type: SPIR-V returns u32x4 (uvec4). AXIOM has uvec4 type? If not, return as tuple (u32, u32, u32, u32) or add uvec4 type. Recommend uvec4 builtin type since DESIGN.md §3.1 already lists it.
workgroup_barrier memory semantics: only WorkgroupMemory? Or also StorageBuffer? Spec both with AcquireRelease for safety.
Capability/extension dedup: multiple subgroup ops needing same capability must emit capability ONCE. Use the existing M1.1 capability-escalation pattern.
